scope: Cradle to gate (A1-A3) with modules C1-C4 and D; A4-A5 and B1-B7 not declared (not modelled — best considered at building/structure level)

notes: MULTI-PRODUCT / CATALOG-SCALE EPD (flagging prominently, per instructions): Table 1 (p.5) plus the version-history log (p.71) jointly declare 76 distinct ready-mix concrete designs (name = family+subcode, e.g. 'N2020PRef', 'S1020PLC1'), each manufactured at 1, 2, or 5 of Hallett's five SA batching plants (Dry Creek, Elizabeth, Mile End, McLaren Vale, Osborne) — footnote p.5: 1 plant=Mile End only, 2 plants=Mile End+Dry Creek, 5 plants=all five. This is far larger than the 1-product EPDs used to lock schema v1.1; the 76-entry products[] array below is a best-effort, ask-shaped structure — please confirm the desired granularity.

GWP-A1A3 SOURCE CHOICE: every product's A1-A3 GWPt/GWPf/GWPb/GWPluluc in this file is read from the Mile End batch-plant results table (Table 25, PDF pp.19-20) — the only plant that manufactures all 76 designs, so it is the single consistent source for a complete catalog. The SAME named design manufactured at other plants has its OWN separately declared (similar but not identical) A1-A3 result: the 24 designs shared by all 5 plants also appear in Dry Creek Table 13 (PDF pp.11-12), Elizabeth Table 19 (PDF pp.16-17), McLaren Vale Table 31 (PDF pp.29-31), and Osborne Table 37 (PDF pp.32-33); the further 20 designs shared with Dry Creek also appear in Table 13. Those plant-specific alternates are NOT reproduced per-product here (would be up to 192 total declared A1-A3 results across all plants) — only Mile End's figures are captured.

C1-C4 + D (end-of-life) HANDLING: the source gives ONE representative result set for modules C1,C2,C3,C4,D (Table 43, PDF p.35, mix 'N3220P S50', density 2332 kg/m3: GWPt C1=14.8, C2=5.96, C3=5.02, C4=8.45, D=-11.6 kg CO2e/m3) and explicitly instructs (p.35-36, worked example for S5010E1150H) that these be linearly scaled per product by (product density / 2332). This file stores the UNSCALED printed representative figure identically for all 76 products (so C1-C4/D are literally identical across every product entry) rather than computing a per-product derived number that isn't itself printed anywhere — scaling factors range roughly 0.77x (NOFINES, 1790 kg/m3) to 1.13x (S5010 SCPLC2, 2580 kg/m3) if precision is needed.

gwp_total.source_page is set to the Mile End A1-A3 table page (19 or 20) for every product; the C1-C4/D figures within the same modules block actually come from PDF p.35 (see note above) — schema has one source_page per gwp_total block, so this is a disclosed simplification, not a hidden one.

COMPRESSIVE STRENGTH STANDARD: unlike the other Hallett/IES worked examples, this document never explicitly cites 'AS 1379' by name. value_mpa / raw_class come from Table 1's explicit Grade(MPa) column (p.5); the AS 1379 standard attribution is inferred from the S-/N- mix code prefixes (Special-class / Normal-class), which is the universal AU ready-mix convention, but is not a verbatim source citation.

'N2010P' / 'N2020P' naming: Table 1 (p.5) explicitly gives Grade=20 MPa for BOTH families despite '10'/'20' appearing in the code — the code encodes grade+slump/pump-designation (e.g. N20-10-P), not grade alone; cross-checked against the Grade column, not a source error.

gwp_a1a3_alt intentionally omitted for all products: per-plant EN15804+A1 tables exist (Dry Creek Table 18, Elizabeth Table 24, Mile End Table 30, McLaren Vale Table 36, Osborne Table 42) giving an alternate-methodology GWP per product, not extracted here to bound scope.

summary_extras intentionally omitted for all products: source reports PERE/PERT/PENRE/PENRT separately with no single combined 'total energy use' figure (same pattern as IES-0029695); net freshwater (FW) is reported per-product in the resource-use tables (e.g. Table 14 Dry Creek, Table 26 Mile End) but was not captured to bound file size.

Verification: 'EPD verification (External): no' is printed alongside a named third-party verifier (start2see Pty Ltd / Rob Rouwette, 'approved by EPD Australasia and The International EPD System') under 'EPD process certification (Internal)' — same pattern as IES-0029695 (Epsten Group): this is a company-wide EPD process certification, not a per-EPD external verifier sign-off.

Full registration: EPD-IES-0009353:003 (revision 3, published 2026-05-04; valid 2023-07-31 to 2028-07-31). Version history (p.71): 001 (2023-07-31) original release = the 24 N-series designs shared across all 5 plants; 002 (2024-10-11) added 20 designs at Dry Creek+Mile End; 003 (2026-05-04, this revision) added 32 further Mile-End-only designs.

Minor source typo: Table 1 (p.5) prints 'Level1' without a space for the S2520 PLC1 Enviro Construct level (all other entries have a space before the level number) — Enviro Construct level is not a schema field, noted for completeness only.

Per-m3 A1A3 outlier check: CLSMPEB (88.9), CLSM3 (72.7), CLSM4 (85.1), CLSM5 (97.5) fall under the schema's [100,650] sanity band — expected, not a data error: CLSM ('Controlled Low Strength Material') is a lean flowable-fill product (grade N/A, cement content near-zero, densities ~2000-2020 kg/m3), not structural concrete, so a low GWP is correct.
